VOLATILE_FUNCTION|Model|B2|Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.|cdd7c55e
WHOLE_COLUMN_RANGE|Model|B3|Formula references an entire column range, which can slow recalculation.|cb562b05

impact_factors.explanation: Issue is on a hidden worksheet, so reviewers can miss it during a walkthrough.
Formula uses higher-risk volatile functions: RAND.

audit_findings.issue_id: VOLATILE_FUNCTION|HiddenInputs|A1|Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.|0b67fd07
audit_findings.message: Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.
audit_findings.rationale: Volatile functions recalculate on every workbook change, which can slow large models and make timing-dependent results harder to reason about.
audit_findings.remediation: Replace volatile functions where possible, isolate them on a dedicated calculation sheet, or document why live recalculation is required.
audit_findings.title: Volatile function

impact_factors.explanation: Control or assumption values are embedded directly in a formula instead of labeled inputs.

audit_findings.rationale: Numeric literals embedded in formulas are harder to audit, reuse, and update than values stored in labeled input cells.
audit_findings.remediation: Move constants into clearly labeled input cells or named ranges and reference those cells from the formula.
audit_findings.title: Hardcoded numeric constant

impact_factors.explanation: Formula only uses TODAY(), which is volatile but usually lower priority than INDIRECT/OFFSET families.

audit_findings.issue_id: VOLATILE_FUNCTION|Model|B2|Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.|cdd7c55e
audit_findings.message: Formula uses volatile functions that can trigger expensive recalculation.
audit_findings.rationale: Volatile functions recalculate on every workbook change, which can slow large models and make timing-dependent results harder to reason about.
audit_findings.remediation: Replace volatile functions where possible, isolate them on a dedicated calculation sheet, or document why live recalculation is required.
audit_findings.title: Volatile function

audit_findings.issue_id: WHOLE_COLUMN_RANGE|Model|B3|Formula references an entire column range, which can slow recalculation.|cb562b05
audit_findings.message: Formula references an entire column range, which can slow recalculation.
audit_findings.rationale: Entire-column references expand to very large ranges and can slow recalculation or hide accidental blanks in large worksheets.
audit_findings.remediation: Restrict the range to the populated rows/columns, or convert the logic to a structured table with explicit bounds.
audit_findings.title: Whole-column range reference

impact_factors.explanation: Formula depends on another workbook file path, version, and availability.

audit_findings.rationale: The formula points at another workbook file, which makes results depend on external file paths, versions, and availability.
audit_findings.remediation: Document the external dependency, confirm the linked file path, or paste values into a controlled input range when the model must be self-contained.
audit_findings.title: External workbook reference

audit_findings.rationale: The stored formula text still contains #REF!, so the workbook may calculate incorrectly even when neighboring cells look fine.
audit_findings.remediation: Repair or remove the broken reference inside the formula, then recalculate downstream dependents after the source range is restored.
audit_findings.title: Broken reference in formula

impact_factors.explanation: Control or assumption values are embedded directly in a formula instead of labeled inputs.

audit_findings.rationale: Numeric literals embedded in formulas are harder to audit, reuse, and update than values stored in labeled input cells.
audit_findings.remediation: Move constants into clearly labeled input cells or named ranges and reference those cells from the formula.
audit_findings.title: Hardcoded numeric constant
